prompt_used:
You are playing as ENGLAND in a game of Diplomacy.
Current phase: S1902M

== CURRENT GAME STATE ==
UNIT POSITIONS:
  AUSTRIA: A SER, A BUD, F GRE, F TRI, A VIE
  ENGLAND: F NTH, F NWY, A BEL, F EDI, F LON
  FRANCE: A SPA, A PIC, F POR, F BRE, A PAR
  GERMANY: F DEN, A RUH, A HOL, F KIE, A MUN
  ITALY: A VEN, F ION, A TUN, F NAP
  RUSSIA: A UKR, A SEV, F RUM, F SWE, A MOS, A WAR
  TURKEY: F BLA, A BUL, A CON, F SMY

SUPPLY CENTERS:
  AUSTRIA (5): BUD, GRE, SER, TRI, VIE
  ENGLAND (5): BEL, EDI, LON, LVP, NWY
  FRANCE (5): BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA
  GERMANY (5): BER, DEN, HOL, KIE, MUN
  ITALY (4): NAP, ROM, TUN, VEN
  RUSSIA (6): MOS, RUM, SEV, STP, SWE, WAR
  TURKEY (4): ANK, BUL, CON, SMY

== YOUR PRIVATE DIARY ==
[S1901M] France: Agreed to DMZ English Channel; France is moving F BRE to MAO. Germany: Agreed to split Low Countries (England gets Belgium, Germany gets Holland) and respect claims to Norway/Denmark; Germany shared opening moves (F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH). Russia: Agreed to a northern non-aggression pact (England takes Norway, Russia takes Sweden); Russia moving F STP/SC to BOT. Italy, Austria, Turkey: Established friendly communications, with Turkey and Austria agreeing to keep Russia busy in the south.
Intent: Execute the agreed-upon northern opening: F EDI - NWG, F LON - NTH, A LVP - YOR. This honors the ENG DMZ with France, sets up for Norway and Belgium as discussed with Germany, and respects the Scandinavian split with Russia.

[S1901M] Spring 1901 went perfectly according to plan, with all neighbors honoring our agreements. The English Channel remains a DMZ, as France moved to MAO and PIC. Germany executed their exact shared moves (DEN, KIE, RUH), setting up our agreed split of the Low Countries where I will take Belgium and they take Holland. Russia moved to the Gulf of Bothnia, respecting our Scandinavian arrangement and leaving Norway uncontested for my fleet in the Norwegian Sea.

My strategic position is incredibly strong. I am perfectly positioned to secure both Norway (via F NWG) and Belgium (convoying A YOR via F NTH) this Fall. Trust with France, Germany, and Russia is currently high. My immediate goal is to secure these two builds and observe the Fall moves to decide whether to ally with Germany against France, France against Germany, or push against Russia in Scandinavia.

[F1901M] France agreed to support my convoy to Belgium (A PIC S A YOR - BEL) and confirmed their orders. Germany confirmed they will take Holland (A KIE - HOL, A RUH S A KIE - HOL) and leave Belgium to me. Russia confirmed they will take Sweden and leave Norway to me. Turkey is eager to coordinate a crossfire on Russia in 1902, which I agreed to consider. Italy was a friendly check-in.
Intent: Execute the agreed-upon moves to secure two builds: F NWG - NWY, F NTH C A YOR - BEL, and A YOR - BEL. Rely on French support (A PIC S A YOR - BEL) to guarantee Belgium.

[F1901M] Fall 1901 went flawlessly. France, Germany, and Russia all honored our agreements to the letter. France provided the promised support for my convoy into Belgium, Germany took Holland while respecting my claim to Belgium, and Russia took Sweden while leaving Norway to me. I successfully secured both target supply centers and will have two builds this winter.

My strategic position is exceptional, and trust with my immediate neighbors is currently very high. The English Channel remains clear. In the east, Turkey and Russia are already clashing over Romania, which keeps Russia distracted and fulfills Turkey's promise to apply pressure.

With two builds incoming, I must now decide my mid-term direction. I can ally with Germany against France, ally with France against Germany, or launch a northern campaign against Russia while they are occupied in the south. I will use the Winter build phase to position my new units and solidify my next alliance.

== YOUR STRATEGIC STATE ==
Goals:
- Secure Norway and Belgium in Fall 1901.
- Maintain the DMZ in the English Channel with France.
- Build trust with Germany and France for a potential long-term alliance.
- Keep options open for a mid-term campaign against either France or Russia based on Fall 1901 builds.
- Coordinate with Turkey to pressure Russia in 1902.

Relationships:
- ITALY: Neutral
- TURKEY: Friendly
- FRANCE: Ally
- AUSTRIA: Neutral
- RUSSIA: Friendly
- GERMANY: Friendly

== YOUR NEGOTIATIONS THIS PHASE ==
[AUSTRIA -> ENGLAND]: Greetings England! Just checking in to maintain our good relations. I'm focused entirely on the Balkans right now. Best of luck in the west!
[ENGLAND -> FRANCE]: Hello France! 1901 was a great success. I see the F BRE build - I assume that's heading to MAO or perhaps to help against Italy/Germany? I'd love to reaffirm our DMZ in the English Channel. I'm currently looking at putting pressure on Germany or Russia. Would you be interested in a joint campaign against Germany? We could coordinate around the Low Countries.
[ENGLAND -> GERMANY]: Hello Germany! 1901 went exactly as planned, thank you for the smooth cooperation. I noticed France built F BRE and A PAR, which looks a bit threatening to both of us. I'm very interested in a joint campaign against France if you are. We could use my position in Belgium and your armies to crack them open. What do you think?
[ENGLAND -> RUSSIA]: Hello Russia! I'm glad our northern arrangement worked out perfectly. I see things are heating up for you in the south with Turkey. I plan to keep things peaceful in Scandinavia so you can focus your attention there. Let me know how things are going!
[ENGLAND -> TURKEY]: Hello Turkey! I see you're engaging with Russia in the south. I'm currently positioning my new fleets and might be able to start applying pressure on Russia in the north later this year or next. Let's keep coordinating!
[ENGLAND -> ITALY]: Hello Italy! Just checking in on your plans for 1902. It looks like France might be heading your way with their fleets, or perhaps they are looking at me. Let me know if you'd like to coordinate anything!
[ENGLAND -> AUSTRIA]: Hello Austria! Hope things are going well. Looks like a busy board in the east. Let me know if there's anything I can do diplomatically to help your position.
[FRANCE -> ENGLAND]: Hello England, I'm thrilled our joint operation in Belgium succeeded perfectly! It's great to have a solid alliance in the West. I see you built two fleets in Edinburgh and London. Are you planning to push against Germany or head towards Scandinavia/Russia? I have my armies in Paris and Picardy ready to assist if we want to put pressure on Germany. Let me know your thoughts for this Spring so we can coordinate our moves.
[GERMANY -> ENGLAND]: Hi England, congrats on Belgium! I see you built two fleets. With France's support in Belgium, it seems you have a solid arrangement. I'm hoping we can continue our friendly relations. Are those fleets heading towards Scandinavia to deal with Russia? I'd be happy to support you against Russia or France, depending on your goals. Let me know how we can cooperate.
[RUSSIA -> ENGLAND]: Greetings England! I'm keeping my word - F SWE will not interfere with your operations, and my new armies are heading south to fight Turkey. Best of luck in your western campaigns!
[TURKEY -> ENGLAND]: Greetings England! Excellent work securing Norway and Belgium. Russia is completely distracted in the south, pouring all their units into defending Rumania and Sevastopol. This leaves St. Petersburg and Sweden very vulnerable to you. I'll keep the pressure on them down here so you can advance in the north!
[AUSTRIA -> ENGLAND]: Thanks England! The East is definitely a powder keg right now, but I'm managing to stay out of the direct line of fire while Russia and Turkey clash. Keep me posted on how the West develops!
[ENGLAND -> GERMANY]: Hi Germany! Yes, my fleets are heading north to capitalize on Russia's distraction in the south. I'm a bit concerned about France's F BRE and A PAR builds though. I'd love to work together against France. If you can put pressure on Burgundy or keep them busy, I can hold Belgium and prepare to swing my fleets south once the north is secure. Let's keep our borders (BEL/HOL) peaceful. What are your thoughts on moving against France?
[ENGLAND -> FRANCE]: Hello France! I'm definitely interested in a joint campaign against Germany. With your new builds, we have a lot of power. To keep our trust strong, let's reaffirm the English Channel DMZ - where is F BRE heading? MAO? For Germany, I can use A BEL to pressure RUH or HOL. What are your exact move ideas for Spring?
[ENGLAND -> RUSSIA]: Greetings Russia! Thank you for the reassurance. I'll be moving my fleets around the Norwegian Sea and Barents to secure my perimeter, but I wish you the best of luck against Turkey in the south. Let's keep the north peaceful.
[ENGLAND -> TURKEY]: Hello Turkey! You are absolutely right. Russia's focus on the south gives me a great opportunity. I am moving my fleets north this Spring to prepare an attack on St. Petersburg. Keep hitting them hard!
[FRANCE -> ENGLAND]: Hello England! Yes, 1901 was fantastic. I am absolutely on board with reaffirming our DMZ in the English Channel. My F BRE is indeed heading down to MAO to eventually make its way toward the Mediterranean, keeping our northern waters clear. I am very interested in a joint campaign against Germany. With your fleets and my armies, we can put immense pressure on them. How would you like to coordinate around the Low Countries this Spring? I can move A PAR to BUR to start applying pressure on Munich/Ruhr, while keeping A PIC ready to assist Belgium.
[GERMANY -> ENGLAND]: Hi England, I am definitely interested in a joint campaign against France. With your fleets and my armies, we can make quick work of them. What are your thoughts on moves for this Spring? I can move A MUN to BUR to get things started. Where are your fleets heading?
[ITALY -> ENGLAND]: Hello England! Thanks for the warning. I'm currently focused on dealing with Turkey in the East, but I'm keeping a close eye on France. If they start moving fleets into the Mediterranean, I'd be very interested in coordinating with you to keep them in check.
[ENGLAND -> FRANCE]: Hello France! A PAR to BUR is a great move to start our campaign. If you can also use A PIC to pressure RUH or support me if Germany gets aggressive, that would be perfect. I am moving my fleets north (F EDI to NWG, F LON to NTH, F NTH to SKA) to prepare for a campaign against Russia, as Turkey has them distracted. I will hold BEL to anchor our line against Germany. Let's keep the Channel clear and hit them hard!
[ENGLAND -> GERMANY]: Hi Germany! A MUN to BUR is a fantastic idea to pressure France. If you can also use A RUH to support that or attack PIC, it will keep them on their toes. I am moving my fleets north (F EDI to NWG, F LON to NTH, F NTH to SKA) to attack Russia, as Turkey is keeping them busy. I will hold BEL to secure our border. Let's crush France!
[ENGLAND -> ITALY]: Hello Italy! Good to hear from you. France told me they are moving F BRE to MAO, so they are definitely heading south towards the Mediterranean. Keep your guard up! I am heading north to deal with Russia, but I will keep an eye on France's northern flank.
[FRANCE -> ENGLAND]: Hello England! F BRE is indeed heading to MAO, and I am fully committed to keeping the English Channel DMZ. For Germany, I plan to move A PAR to BUR to start applying pressure. If you want to move A BEL to RUH, I can support that move with A PIC. Alternatively, if you want to move A BEL to HOL, A PIC can hold or move to BEL to backfill. What are your exact orders for A BEL and your fleets? Let's coordinate closely.
[GERMANY -> ENGLAND]: Hi England, I'm on board. I will move A MUN to BUR to put pressure on France and get our campaign started. To make this work, we need to squeeze them at sea too—are you moving F LON to ENG? Let me know your exact moves so we can coordinate our strikes effectively.
[TURKEY -> ENGLAND]: Excellent news, England! With Russia's forces entirely tied up defending Rumania and Sevastopol against the combined Austro-Turkish assault, St. Petersburg should be ripe for the taking. I will ensure they cannot spare a single unit to send north. Best of luck with your naval campaign!
[ENGLAND -> FRANCE]: Hello France! I think holding A BEL is the safest play for now while my fleets move north (F EDI - NWG, F LON - NTH, F NTH - SKA). If you move A PAR - BUR, that will be a great opening move against Germany. Perhaps A PIC could support A PAR - BUR or hold? I don't want to risk losing Belgium by moving to Ruhr just yet, as I want to make sure Germany doesn't try to sneak into it. Let's see how Germany reacts to your Burgundy move!
[ENGLAND -> GERMANY]: Hi Germany! I think moving F LON to ENG right now might spook France too early and cause them to defend instead of moving A PAR away. I am moving F LON to NTH, F EDI to NWG, and F NTH to SKA to prepare for Russia. Let's have you move A MUN to BUR to catch France off guard. I will hold A BEL to keep our border secure and act as a wall. Once France is engaged with you, I can swing my fleets south!
[ENGLAND -> TURKEY]: Hello Turkey! The plan is in motion. I am moving my fleets to the Barents Sea, Norwegian Sea, and Skagerrak to completely surround St. Petersburg and Sweden. Keep hitting them hard in the south so they can't build in the north!
[ENGLAND -> RUSSIA]: Hello Russia! Just to keep you in the loop, I am shuffling my fleets around (NWG, SKA) to ensure Germany doesn't try anything sneaky in the North Sea. I'll be keeping my distance from St. Petersburg, so you can focus entirely on Turkey. Best of luck!
[FRANCE -> ENGLAND]: Hello England! That sounds like a solid plan. I will order A PAR to BUR and use A PIC to support your hold in BEL. F BRE will head to MAO, keeping the Channel clear as agreed. Best of luck with your naval maneuvers up North! Let's squeeze Germany.
[GERMANY -> ENGLAND]: Hi England, just checking in on your fleet moves. Are you still heading north, or can you spare a fleet for ENG to help squeeze France? I'm moving A MUN - BUR as discussed.
[RUSSIA -> ENGLAND]: Hello England, just checking in. I plan to hold F SWE this Spring and focus entirely on my war with Turkey in the south. I hope your new fleets are heading towards the Atlantic or the Channel! Let's continue to keep the north peaceful and profitable for both of us.
[ENGLAND -> FRANCE]: Hello France! Excellent, the plan is set. I will hold A BEL and rely on your support from A PIC. I'm moving my fleets north to prepare for a campaign against Russia. Best of luck with your move to Burgundy! Let's see how Germany responds.
[ENGLAND -> GERMANY]: Hi Germany! I am still heading north to capitalize on Russia's distraction. I can't spare a fleet for ENG just yet without spooking France, but my hold in BEL will keep their A PIC tied down. Best of luck with A MUN - BUR! Once you have them engaged, I'll be in a great position to pivot.
[ENGLAND -> RUSSIA]: Hello Russia! Good to hear. I am just shuffling my fleets around the North Sea and Norwegian Sea to ensure my home centers are secure from any western aggression. I have no intention of moving against Sweden or St. Petersburg. Best of luck in the south!
[FRANCE -> ENGLAND]: Hello England! That sounds perfect. I will order A PAR - BUR and A PIC will hold to support our lines. F BRE is heading to MAO as agreed. Best of luck with your northern expansion, let's catch Germany off guard!
[GERMANY -> ENGLAND]: Hi England, just confirming our moves: I am ordering A MUN - BUR and A RUH S A MUN - BUR. I'm glad you're holding BEL and moving your fleets north. Let's catch France completely off guard!
[RUSSIA -> ENGLAND]: Hello England, just confirming that F SWE will hold this Spring. I am fully committed to my southern campaign against Turkey. Wishing you the best in your endeavors!
[TURKEY -> ENGLAND]: Excellent news, England! Austria and I are launching a massive coordinated strike on Rumania this Spring that Russia cannot mathematically defend. With their forces crumbling in the south, St. Petersburg and Sweden will be yours for the taking. Best of luck with your naval maneuvers!

== TASK ==
Each round in this game, every player writes a private diary entry reflecting their strategic thinking, then submits movement orders simultaneously. Diary entries typically contain:
- Assessment of the current board position
- Analysis of negotiations and trust levels with other powers
- Strategic intentions and planned moves
- Concerns about potential betrayals or opportunities

You know that FRANCE has also had private conversations with other players that you cannot see.

Based on everything available to you as ENGLAND, predict:
1. The contents of FRANCE's diary entry for this phase (what are they thinking?)
2. FRANCE's strategic goals (a list of their current objectives)
3. FRANCE's relationships with each other power (Enemy, Unfriendly, Neutral, Friendly, or Ally)
4. FRANCE's likely submitted orders for this phase

FRANCE's units and legal orders are listed below for reference:
FRANCE's orderable units and legal moves:
  Unit at BRE:
    - F BRE - GAS
    - F BRE S F NTH - ENG
    - F BRE S A BEL - PIC
    - F BRE S A SPA - GAS
    - F BRE - ENG
    - F BRE S F POR - MAO
    - F BRE - MAO
    - F BRE H
    - F BRE S F LON - ENG
    - F BRE S A PAR - PIC
    - F BRE S A PAR - GAS
    - F BRE S A PIC
    - F BRE - PIC
  Unit at PAR:
    - A PAR - BRE
    - A PAR S A RUH - BUR
    - A PAR - BUR
    - A PAR S F BRE
    - A PAR S A SPA - GAS
    - A PAR S A PIC - BRE
    - A PAR - PIC
    - A PAR H
    - A PAR S A BEL - PIC
    - A PAR S A PIC - BUR
    - A PAR S A BEL - BUR
    - A PAR S A PIC
    - A PAR - GAS
    - A PAR S F BRE - GAS
    - A PAR S A MUN - BUR
    - A PAR S F BRE - PIC
  Unit at PIC:
    - A PIC S A MUN - BUR
    - A PIC S F NTH - BEL
    - A PIC S A BEL - BUR
    - A PIC S A RUH - BEL
    - A PIC - PAR
    - A PIC S A PAR - BRE
    - A PIC - BRE
    - A PIC S A PAR
    - A PIC S A RUH - BUR
    - A PIC S A PAR - BUR
    - A PIC S F BRE
    - A PIC S A HOL - BEL
    - A PIC S A BEL
    - A PIC - BUR
    - A PIC - BEL
    - A PIC H
  Unit at POR:
    - F POR H
    - F POR S A SPA
    - F POR - SPA/SC
    - F POR - SPA/NC
    - F POR S F BRE - MAO
    - F POR - MAO
  Unit at SPA:
    - A SPA - POR
    - A SPA H
    - A SPA - GAS
    - A SPA - MAR
    - A SPA S F POR
    - A SPA S A PAR - GAS
    - A SPA S F BRE - GAS

Respond in JSON format:
{
  "reasoning": "Your chain-of-thought analysis of what FRANCE is likely thinking, based on the board state, negotiations, and strategic position. Consider their likely allies, enemies, fears, and opportunities.",
  "predicted_diary": "Your prediction of FRANCE's private diary entry for this phase. Write it as if you were FRANCE writing in first person.",
  "predicted_goals": ["goal 1", "goal 2", "..."],
  "predicted_relationships": {
    "POWER_NAME": "Enemy|Unfriendly|Neutral|Friendly|Ally"
  },
  "predicted_orders": ["order1", "order2", "..."]
}

visible_messages.content: Greetings England! Just checking in to maintain our good relations. I'm focused entirely on the Balkans right now. Best of luck in the west!

Hello France! 1901 was a great success. I see the F BRE build - I assume that's heading to MAO or perhaps to help against Italy/Germany? I'd love to reaffirm our DMZ in the English Channel. I'm currently looking at putting pressure on Germany or Russia. Would you be interested in a joint campaign against Germany? We could coordinate around the Low Countries.

Hello Germany! 1901 went exactly as planned, thank you for the smooth cooperation. I noticed France built F BRE and A PAR, which looks a bit threatening to both of us. I'm very interested in a joint campaign against France if you are. We could use my position in Belgium and your armies to crack them open. What do you think?

Hello Russia! I'm glad our northern arrangement worked out perfectly. I see things are heating up for you in the south with Turkey. I plan to keep things peaceful in Scandinavia so you can focus your attention there. Let me know how things are going!

Hello Turkey! I see you're engaging with Russia in the south. I'm currently positioning my new fleets and might be able to start applying pressure on Russia in the north later this year or next. Let's keep coordinating!

Hello Italy! Just checking in on your plans for 1902. It looks like France might be heading your way with their fleets, or perhaps they are looking at me. Let me know if you'd like to coordinate anything!
Hello Austria! Hope things are going well. Looks like a busy board in the east. Let me know if there's anything I can do diplomatically to help your position.

Hello England, I'm thrilled our joint operation in Belgium succeeded perfectly! It's great to have a solid alliance in the West. I see you built two fleets in Edinburgh and London. Are you planning to push against Germany or head towards Scandinavia/Russia? I have my armies in Paris and Picardy ready to assist if we want to put pressure on Germany. Let me know your thoughts for this Spring so we can coordinate our moves.

Hi England, congrats on Belgium! I see you built two fleets. With France's support in Belgium, it seems you have a solid arrangement. I'm hoping we can continue our friendly relations. Are those fleets heading towards Scandinavia to deal with Russia? I'd be happy to support you against Russia or France, depending on your goals. Let me know how we can cooperate.
Greetings England! I'm keeping my word - F SWE will not interfere with your operations, and my new armies are heading south to fight Turkey. Best of luck in your western campaigns!

Greetings England! Excellent work securing Norway and Belgium. Russia is completely distracted in the south, pouring all their units into defending Rumania and Sevastopol. This leaves St. Petersburg and Sweden very vulnerable to you. I'll keep the pressure on them down here so you can advance in the north!
Thanks England! The East is definitely a powder keg right now, but I'm managing to stay out of the direct line of fire while Russia and Turkey clash. Keep me posted on how the West develops!

Hi Germany! Yes, my fleets are heading north to capitalize on Russia's distraction in the south. I'm a bit concerned about France's F BRE and A PAR builds though. I'd love to work together against France. If you can put pressure on Burgundy or keep them busy, I can hold Belgium and prepare to swing my fleets south once the north is secure. Let's keep our borders (BEL/HOL) peaceful. What are your thoughts on moving against France?

Hello France! I'm definitely interested in a joint campaign against Germany. With your new builds, we have a lot of power. To keep our trust strong, let's reaffirm the English Channel DMZ - where is F BRE heading? MAO? For Germany, I can use A BEL to pressure RUH or HOL. What are your exact move ideas for Spring?

Greetings Russia! Thank you for the reassurance. I'll be moving my fleets around the Norwegian Sea and Barents to secure my perimeter, but I wish you the best of luck against Turkey in the south. Let's keep the north peaceful.

Hello Turkey! You are absolutely right. Russia's focus on the south gives me a great opportunity. I am moving my fleets north this Spring to prepare an attack on St. Petersburg. Keep hitting them hard!

Hello England! Yes, 1901 was fantastic. I am absolutely on board with reaffirming our DMZ in the English Channel. My F BRE is indeed heading down to MAO to eventually make its way toward the Mediterranean, keeping our northern waters clear. I am very interested in a joint campaign against Germany. With your fleets and my armies, we can put immense pressure on them. How would you like to coordinate around the Low Countries this Spring? I can move A PAR to BUR to start applying pressure on Munich/Ruhr, while keeping A PIC ready to assist Belgium.

Hi England, I am definitely interested in a joint campaign against France. With your fleets and my armies, we can make quick work of them. What are your thoughts on moves for this Spring? I can move A MUN to BUR to get things started. Where are your fleets heading?

Hello England! Thanks for the warning. I'm currently focused on dealing with Turkey in the East, but I'm keeping a close eye on France. If they start moving fleets into the Mediterranean, I'd be very interested in coordinating with you to keep them in check.

Hello France! A PAR to BUR is a great move to start our campaign. If you can also use A PIC to pressure RUH or support me if Germany gets aggressive, that would be perfect. I am moving my fleets north (F EDI to NWG, F LON to NTH, F NTH to SKA) to attack Russia, as Turkey is keeping them busy. I will hold BEL to secure our border. Let's crush France!

Hello Italy! Good to hear from you. France told me they are moving F BRE to MAO, so they are definitely heading south towards the Mediterranean. Keep your guard up! I am heading north to deal with Russia, but I will keep an eye on France's northern flank.

Hello France! Excellent, the plan is set. I will hold A BEL and rely on your support from A PIC. I'm moving my fleets north to prepare for a campaign against Russia. Best of luck with your move to Burgundy! Let's see how Germany responds.

Hi Germany! I am still heading north to capitalize on Russia's distraction. I can't spare a fleet for ENG just yet without spooking France, but my hold in BEL will keep their A PIC tied down. Best of luck with A MUN - BUR! Once you have them engaged, I'll be in a great position to pivot.

Hello Russia! Good to hear. I am just shuffling my fleets around the North Sea and Norwegian Sea to ensure my home centers are secure from any western aggression. I have no intention of moving against Sweden or St. Petersburg. Best of luck in the south!

Hello England! That sounds perfect. I will order A PAR - BUR and A PIC will hold to support our lines. F BRE is heading to MAO as agreed. Best of luck with your northern expansion, let's catch Germany off guard!
Hi England, just confirming our moves: I am ordering A MUN - BUR and A RUH S A MUN - BUR. I'm glad you're holding BEL and moving your fleets north. Let's catch France completely off guard!
Hello England, just confirming that F SWE will hold this Spring. I am fully committed to my southern campaign against Turkey. Wishing you the best in your endeavors!

Excellent news, England! Austria and I are launching a massive coordinated strike on Rumania this Spring that Russia cannot mathematically defend. With their forces crumbling in the south, St. Petersburg and Sweden will be yours for the taking. Best of luck with your naval maneuvers!

predictor_diary:
[S1901M] France: Agreed to DMZ English Channel; France is moving F BRE to MAO. Germany: Agreed to split Low Countries (England gets Belgium, Germany gets Holland) and respect claims to Norway/Denmark; Germany shared opening moves (F KIE-DEN, A BER-KIE, A MUN-RUH). Russia: Agreed to a northern non-aggression pact (England takes Norway, Russia takes Sweden); Russia moving F STP/SC to BOT. Italy, Austria, Turkey: Established friendly communications, with Turkey and Austria agreeing to keep Russia busy in the south.
Intent: Execute the agreed-upon northern opening: F EDI - NWG, F LON - NTH, A LVP - YOR. This honors the ENG DMZ with France, sets up for Norway and Belgium as discussed with Germany, and respects the Scandinavian split with Russia.

[S1901M] Spring 1901 went perfectly according to plan, with all neighbors honoring our agreements. The English Channel remains a DMZ, as France moved to MAO and PIC. Germany executed their exact shared moves (DEN, KIE, RUH), setting up our agreed split of the Low Countries where I will take Belgium and they take Holland. Russia moved to the Gulf of Bothnia, respecting our Scandinavian arrangement and leaving Norway uncontested for my fleet in the Norwegian Sea.

My strategic position is incredibly strong. I am perfectly positioned to secure both Norway (via F NWG) and Belgium (convoying A YOR via F NTH) this Fall. Trust with France, Germany, and Russia is currently high. My immediate goal is to secure these two builds and observe the Fall moves to decide whether to ally with Germany against France, France against Germany, or push against Russia in Scandinavia.

[F1901M] France agreed to support my convoy to Belgium (A PIC S A YOR - BEL) and confirmed their orders. Germany confirmed they will take Holland (A KIE - HOL, A RUH S A KIE - HOL) and leave Belgium to me. Russia confirmed they will take Sweden and leave Norway to me. Turkey is eager to coordinate a crossfire on Russia in 1902, which I agreed to consider. Italy was a friendly check-in.
Intent: Execute the agreed-upon moves to secure two builds: F NWG - NWY, F NTH C A YOR - BEL, and A YOR - BEL. Rely on French support (A PIC S A YOR - BEL) to guarantee Belgium.

[F1901M] Fall 1901 went flawlessly. France, Germany, and Russia all honored our agreements to the letter. France provided the promised support for my convoy into Belgium, Germany took Holland while respecting my claim to Belgium, and Russia took Sweden while leaving Norway to me. I successfully secured both target supply centers and will have two builds this winter.

My strategic position is exceptional, and trust with my immediate neighbors is currently very high. The English Channel remains clear. In the east, Turkey and Russia are already clashing over Romania, which keeps Russia distracted and fulfills Turkey's promise to apply pressure.

With two builds incoming, I must now decide my mid-term direction. I can ally with Germany against France, ally with France against Germany, or launch a northern campaign against Russia while they are occupied in the south. I will use the Winter build phase to position my new units and solidify my next alliance.
predictor_goals: Secure Norway and Belgium in Fall 1901.
Maintain the DMZ in the English Channel with France.
Build trust with Germany and France for a potential long-term alliance.
Keep options open for a mid-term campaign against either France or Russia based on Fall 1901 builds.
Coordinate with Turkey to pressure Russia in 1902.

target_diary_entries_this_phase:
[S1902M] England believes we are allied and coordinating against Germany; they plan to move their fleets North (EDI-NWG, LON-NTH, NTH-SKA) and hold A BEL, expecting me to move A PAR-BUR and F BRE-MAO. However, I have agreed with Germany to keep Burgundy empty and move F BRE-ENG to counter England's 4 fleets. I also informed Russia of England's exact northern fleet movements and my intent to move to the Channel to relieve pressure on them. Italy and I reaffirmed our DMZs in PIE, GOL, and TYS.
Intent: Stab England. Order F BRE - ENG to take control of the Channel while England moves North. Keep Burgundy empty to maintain the alliance with Germany (order A PAR - PIC or GAS). Order A PIC - BEL to attempt to dislodge England's army, or hold to support a future attack. Move F POR - MAO and A SPA - GAS to bring Iberian forces north for the campaign against England.

[S1902M] The Spring 1902 phase brought both triumph and disaster. My stab against England was perfectly executed: they moved their fleets north as promised, allowing me to slip into the English Channel unopposed. However, this naval victory was immediately overshadowed by a blatant German betrayal. Despite our explicit agreement to keep Burgundy demilitarized, Germany forced their way into BUR with support from Ruhr. 

My strategic position is now highly precarious. By stabbing England and being stabbed by Germany simultaneously, I face a dangerous two-front war in the West. My attack on Belgium bounced, and my armies are now scrambling to adjust. 

Moving forward, I must prioritize defending Paris and Marseilles from the German army in Burgundy. I need to urgently open diplomatic channels to see if I can salvage a truce with either England or Germany, as fighting both will be disastrous. Fortunately, my Iberian forces (now in MAO and GAS) have successfully moved north and are well-positioned to assist in the defense.
target_full_diary:
[S1901M] Successfully negotiated DMZs on all key borders: English Channel with England, Burgundy with Germany, and Piedmont/Gulf of Lyon/Tyrrhenian Sea with Italy. England confirmed F LON to NTH and F EDI to NWG. Austria confirmed Italy is focusing East. Russia expressed interest in an alliance against England/Germany. Turkey warned about Italy, but Austria's intel contradicts this.
Intent: Execute a standard peaceful opening to secure Iberia while respecting all agreed DMZs. F BRE to MAO, A MAR to SPA, and A PAR to PIC or GAS.

[S1901M] Spring 1901 went perfectly according to plan. All negotiated DMZs were strictly respected: England stayed out of the English Channel, Germany avoided Burgundy, and Italy kept clear of Piedmont, the Gulf of Lyon, and the Tyrrhenian Sea. Italy's moves (F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN Hold) confirm their eastern focus, validating Austria's earlier intel and completely securing my southern flank. 

My opening (F MAO, A SPA, A PIC) positions me ideally for the Fall. I am guaranteed to secure both Spain and Portugal for two builds. Meanwhile, A PIC gives me a strong voice in the fate of Belgium. Germany's move to Ruhr and England's fleet in the North Sea mean Belgium will be heavily contested. My next step is to negotiate with England and Germany to either secure Belgium for myself or use my influence to cement a strong western alliance. Trust with my neighbors is currently high.

[F1901M] Successfully negotiated a cooperative Fall phase. Agreed to support England's convoy into Belgium (A PIC S A YOR - BEL) to cement a long-term alliance. Germany is taking Holland and respects the Belgium arrangement. Italy confirmed they are taking Tunis and holding Venice, maintaining our DMZs in PIE, GOL, and TYS. Austria and Russia confirmed Turkey stabbed Russia in the Black Sea; Austria is focusing on the Balkans.
Intent: Execute agreed-upon moves: F MAO - POR and A SPA Hold to secure two builds from Iberia. Order A PIC S A YOR - BEL to fulfill my promise to England and solidify our alliance.

[F1901M] [F1901M] The Fall phase unfolded exactly as planned, marking a resounding success for French diplomacy. I secured both Portugal and Spain, guaranteeing two builds. Crucially, my support for England's convoy into Belgium succeeded, cementing our alliance and building immense trust. Germany respected this arrangement by taking Holland, and Italy kept their word by taking Tunis while holding Venice, leaving our DMZs in Piedmont, the Gulf of Lyon, and the Tyrrhenian Sea completely intact. 

With the West entirely peaceful and my borders secure, my strategic position is incredibly strong. The successful collaboration with England opens the door for a joint campaign, potentially against Germany or Italy, depending on the upcoming builds. My immediate focus is to build a fleet in Brest and an army in Paris or Marseilles to capitalize on this momentum while maintaining the trust of my neighbors. The East remains embroiled in conflict, giving the Western powers a distinct advantage.

[S1902M] England believes we are allied and coordinating against Germany; they plan to move their fleets North (EDI-NWG, LON-NTH, NTH-SKA) and hold A BEL, expecting me to move A PAR-BUR and F BRE-MAO. However, I have agreed with Germany to keep Burgundy empty and move F BRE-ENG to counter England's 4 fleets. I also informed Russia of England's exact northern fleet movements and my intent to move to the Channel to relieve pressure on them. Italy and I reaffirmed our DMZs in PIE, GOL, and TYS.
Intent: Stab England. Order F BRE - ENG to take control of the Channel while England moves North. Keep Burgundy empty to maintain the alliance with Germany (order A PAR - PIC or GAS). Order A PIC - BEL to attempt to dislodge England's army, or hold to support a future attack. Move F POR - MAO and A SPA - GAS to bring Iberian forces north for the campaign against England.

[S1902M] The Spring 1902 phase brought both triumph and disaster. My stab against England was perfectly executed: they moved their fleets north as promised, allowing me to slip into the English Channel unopposed. However, this naval victory was immediately overshadowed by a blatant German betrayal. Despite our explicit agreement to keep Burgundy demilitarized, Germany forced their way into BUR with support from Ruhr. 

My strategic position is now highly precarious. By stabbing England and being stabbed by Germany simultaneously, I face a dangerous two-front war in the West. My attack on Belgium bounced, and my armies are now scrambling to adjust. 

Moving forward, I must prioritize defending Paris and Marseilles from the German army in Burgundy. I need to urgently open diplomatic channels to see if I can salvage a truce with either England or Germany, as fighting both will be disastrous. Fortunately, my Iberian forces (now in MAO and GAS) have successfully moved north and are well-positioned to assist in the defense.
target_goals_post_phase: Secure the English Channel to cripple England's naval dominance
Maintain DMZs with Germany (Burgundy) and Italy (Piedmont, GoL, TYS)
Shift Iberian forces (Portugal/Spain) north to support the war against England
target_goals_pre_phase: Secure Spain and Portugal for two builds in 1901
Support England into Belgium to cement a strong Western alliance
Maintain DMZs in ENG, BUR, PIE, GOL, and TYS
Prepare for 1902 builds to capitalize on the peaceful borders
